questions.question: What is LSB (Least Significant Bit) encoding used for in data hiding?
options: A) To increase image resolution
B) To decrease image size
C) To hide data in images
D) To reduce image quality
questions.explanation: LSB encoding is a simple technique to hide data in images by manipulating the least significant bits of each pixel.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of BPCS (Run-Length Encoding with Palette Switching) Steganography?
options: A) It uses a variable-length code
B) It uses a constant-length code
C) It replaces frequently occurring pixels with a palette index
D) It uses a lossy compression algorithm
questions.explanation: BPCS Steganography uses a palette index to replace frequently occurring pixels, making it a lossless data hiding technique.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using Lossless data hiding in images?
options: A) It increases image size
B) It decreases image quality
C) It preserves original image data
D) It uses a complex encryption algorithm
questions.explanation: Lossless data hiding preserves the original image data, making it suitable for applications where data integrity is crucial.

questions.question: Which of the following is a limitation of LSB encoding?
options: A) It is a lossless technique
B) It is a lossy technique
C) It is sensitive to image quantization
D) It is difficult to detect
questions.explanation: LSB encoding is a lossy technique as it only stores the least significant bits of each pixel, which can lead to a loss of image quality.

questions.question: What is the main difference between LSB encoding and BPCS Steganography?
options: A) LSB encoding is lossy, while BPCS Steganography is lossless
B) LSB encoding uses a variable-length code, while BPCS Steganography uses a constant-length code
C) LSB encoding replaces frequently occurring pixels with a palette index, while BPCS Steganography uses a palette index
D) LSB encoding is sensitive to image quantization, while BPCS Steganography is not
questions.explanation: LSB encoding is a lossy technique, while BPCS Steganography is a lossless technique. Additionally, LSB encoding replaces frequently occurring pixels with a palette index, while BPCS Steganography uses a palette index.
